title: Scorer
description: A deterministic grader. Scorers consume `(Sample, SolverOutput)` and produce a Score with no LLM call. They are cheap, cacheable, and parallel-safe. LLM-graded counterparts are Judges (see judge.json) — the split is deliberate (research/12 §1).

kind.description: Scorer implementation kind. `code-test` runs the candidate's output through a test suite; `ast-diff` and `taint-flow` are tree-sitter / dataflow grades unique to this harness (research/12 §3.5); `custom-*` allow user-supplied scorer code in the matching language.

params.description: Scorer-kind-specific parameters. For `regex` this carries the pattern; for `json-schema` the embedded schema; for `code-test` the test harness and language; etc. Open by design.
